pick.rationale: Alabama holds a substantial advantage in this 4/13 matchup, ranking 16th-17th nationally in efficiency metrics compared to Hofstra's 87th-92nd range, creating roughly a 16-point adjusted efficiency margin gap. The Crimson Tide feature an elite top-3 offense that should exploit Hofstra's below-average defense ranked 96th nationally, while the SEC pedigree provides a significant strength of schedule advantage over the CAA champion. Although 13-seeds pull upsets approximately 20% of the time in March Madness and Hofstra's 24-10 record demonstrates conference tournament success, Alabama's superior talent level, offensive firepower, and power conference experience should prove decisive in this Round of 64 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Alabama vs Hofstra - 4/13 Seed Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Alabama's elite offense and substantial efficiency advantage over Hofstra makes them the clear favorite in this 4/13 seed matchup.
reasoningStep.evidence: Alabama ranks 16th-17th nationally (KenPom/Torvik) with a +25.64 adjusted efficiency margin and 3rd-ranked offense (129.4 adj_o), while Hofstra ranks 87th-92nd with a +9.46 margin and below-average ratings on both ends. The 16-point efficiency gap and SEC vs CAA strength of schedule disparity mirror other 4/13 matchups in this bracket where the 4-seed was favored at 76-82% confidence.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

modelTrace.content:
The web searches are returning empty results. This is fine - I have enough data from the ratings lookup to make an informed prediction.

Let me analyze what I know:

Alabama (4-seed):
- Ranked 16th-17th nationally in both KenPom and Torvik
- 23-9 record from the SEC
- Elite offense: 3rd nationally in adjusted offensive efficiency (129.4)
- Average defense: 67th in adjusted defensive efficiency (102.8)
- Adjusted efficiency margin: +25.64

Hofstra (13-seed):
- Ranked 87th-92nd nationally
- 24-10 record from the CAA
- Below average offense: 89th-98th in adjusted offensive efficiency (113.2-114.6)
- Below average defense: 96th in adjusted defensive efficiency (105.0)
- Adjusted efficiency margin: +9.46

The gap is substantial - roughly 16 points in adjusted efficiency margin. Alabama's elite offense should be able to score efficiently against Hofstra's below-average defense. While Alabama's defense is only average, Hofstra's offense is not potent enough to exploit that weakness consistently.

This is a 4/13 matchup, and historically 13-seeds win about 20% of these games. However, the efficiency gap here is larger than typical 4/13 matchups, and Alabama's SEC pedigree provides a significant strength of schedule advantage.

Looking at prior picks in this bracket, I see similar 4/13 matchups:
- r64-east-4: Kansas (4) over California Baptist (13) at 78% confidence
- r64-west-4: Arkansas (4) over Hawai'i (13) at 76% confidence
- r64-south-4: Nebraska (4) over Troy (13) at 82% confidence

Alabama vs Hofstra has a similar or larger efficiency gap than these matchups. Nebraska had a 24-point EM gap over Troy, Alabama has about a 16-point gap over Hofstra. Kansas and Arkansas had similar profiles.

I should pick Alabama with confidence in the 75-80% range, similar to the other 4/13 picks in this bracket.

Let me finalize my prediction.
